name: SHARP - Sheriff's Alarm Registration Program (for Pinellas County)

metadata.description:
10-23-20: Your permit number is 61285

10-22-20 (LZ)
A 2009 Pinellas County ordinance requires home and business owners to register their alarm systems with the Pinellas County Sheriff’s Office. The ordinance has also implemented a fine schedule for any false alarms which may occur at that home or business.

The Sheriff’s Alarm Registration Program or “SHARP” has been established for home and business owners to register their alarm protected properties on-line or by filling out a registration form and mailing it to:
SHARP
Pinellas County Sheriff’s Office
P.O. Box 2500
Largo, FL 33779-2500

There is no registration fee to sign up with SHARP.

Alarm subscribers registered with SHARP will receive warnings for the first two false alarms within a revolving year. Registered alarm owners who experience more than two false alarms in one year will be subject to fines as identified below.

Home and business owners who do not register their alarm systems with SHARP do not receive any warnings and will be subject to fines as identified in the schedule below.

Even registered alarm owners who experience more than two false alarm events in one year may also be subject to fines, as identified below, if the false alarms continue.

**Fines for Registered Alarms**

Three false alarms $30
Four $100
Five $200
Six $250
Seven and above $300

**Fines for Non-Registered Alarms**

One $80
Two $160
Three $320
Four and Above $500

​Fines will be collected by the Sheriff’s Office after the alarm subscriber is notified following a violation.

An appeals process is available to both registered and non-registered subscribers.

Well over 90% of the alarm calls investigated by deputy sheriffs are false alarms. The purpose of the ordinance is to reduce the number of false alarms in the sheriff’s jurisdiction, and the time it takes for deputy sheriffs to respond to them.

Fewer false alarms will contribute to the efficiency of deputy sheriffs on patrol as they are better able to spend their time resp

David Lester will be in charge of Insect and Pest Control at WPT

We will be held responsible for once monthly outside spraying of the buildings for all rodents and insects. As of 8/14/19, waiting for quote from David Lester. 

Residents will be responsible for spraying the inside of their units. David will be providing a quote to us for the residents. We will refer his business to our residents. 

Need to know how David Lester will be invoicing us. We need to be invoiced monthly and will either put him on DD or check. For personal use in the past, we have paid him in cash. Please make him aware that we will not be paying in cash. 

As a courtesy, David Lester is spraying the inside of new move-in 2786 Chapman's unit. In her first week of living in her unit, she had insects on the ground. I hope that makes a good first impression. *David made it seem like he was doing this spray free of charge, if so, lets "tip" him a little extra on his first invoice. 

David Lester is also taking care of the wasp nest located outside of 2798 Canora's unit. *David made it seem like he was doing this spray free of charge, if so, lets "tip" him a little extra on his first invoice. 

David Lester is aware that he will be responsible for spraying the inside of a unit prior to a new move-in. Once we know someone is moving in, please schedule the inside spray a couple days prior to the move-in date. 

We need to come up with an Insect and Pest Control protocol for current residents and also update the lease to reflect this for future residents. Something that states that the resident is responsible for providing proof that they currently have a pest/insect control spraying their unit. If no proof is provided, we will charge an inside spraying fee to their ledger and have David spray the insides of their units.
